candidates.reasoning: The combination of semi-arid soil, scrubby green vegetation, and distant volcanic-style mountains is highly characteristic of the Mexican plateau. The agricultural patterns and dirt road quality are common in rural central Mexico.
The terrain and vegetation are very similar to the highlands of Guatemala, where semi-arid valleys exist between volcanic peaks. It shares the same general landscape profile as southern Mexico.
The dry, scrubby landscape and soil color are reminiscent of the Meseta Central in Spain, though the specific vegetation and mountain silhouettes are slightly more characteristic of the Americas.

evidence: Semi-arid soil with light brown/grey hue
High sun angle suggesting low to mid-latitude

candidates.reasoning: The combination of arid soil, scrubby Prosopis-like (mesquite) trees in the background, and the specific appearance of the low-lying agricultural crops is highly characteristic of the Mexican plateau or northern highlands. Similar vegetation exists in the Southwestern US, but the agricultural layout is more typical of rural Mexico.

The xeric shrubland and presence of drought-resistant trees are consistent with the Sonoran or Chihuahuan desert regions of Arizona and New Mexico. Botanically, this is nearly indistinguishable from Northern Mexico without further specific species identification.
